behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at the handoff between classifier request-builder and API client payload serialization, not just one pure function. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: real network is not required; a capturing in-process client can expose missing labels. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no dependence on model judgment and no multi-step agent pipeline. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: if request assembly is a single pure function, Unit could be enough. TOP2-CONTRAST (runner-up Unit): behavior text is about what is actually sent to the external API, so module-to-client wiring must be exercised. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text does not require real HTTP/OS/subprocess.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a fake API client that records outbound payload; invoke classify with a known taxonomy and assert recorded request contains the full label set exactly once before call dispatch.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at response-handoff boundary (provider response -> module output contract). REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess needed; stubbed provider response reproduces it. LLM-DEP: correctness here is post-processing guardrail, not model quality. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: if invalid-label mapping is isolated in a pure normalizer, Unit may suffice. TOP2-CONTRAST (runner-up Unit): behavior text targets module behavior when API returns data, so response integration path should be exercised. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior is independent of whether model chose well; it only checks taxonomy validation.
behaviors.test_description: Use an in-process fake provider returning an out-of-taxonomy label; call classifier and assert public return value is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is a deterministic normalization function property for fixed input. No module boundary is required to trigger failure. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none apply. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: if normalization implicitly depends on shared mutable config loaded via another module, Integration may be needed. TOP2-CONTRAST (runner-up Integration): behavior text explicitly fixes identical raw responses and checks normalization determinism, which is local logic.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization logic repeatedly with the same raw response fixture and assert identical output each run across multiple iterations.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: behavior explicitly depends on repeated live model calls and consistency of provider outputs; stubs hide real model variance and provider-side drift. REAL-INFRA exists too, but core risk is model/API behavior, so Agentic beats System. MULTI-STEP: repeated calls are parallel repetitions of one agentic step, not a chained agent pipeline. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: if this only compares cached raw provider payload equality and local mapping, Integration might be enough. TOP2-CONTRAST (runner-up System): system-level HTTP realism is necessary but insufficient; the target failure is model/output consistency from the real AI provider.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated real provider calls for identical input under controlled settings; record raw responses and final labels; assert label stability when raw provider output is unchanged and attribute deviations to provider output changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: risk is boundary between tokenizer/budget policy and dispatch gate (state handoff before send). REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced with in-process tokenizer and fake dispatch client; no real HTTP needed. LLM-DEP: no dependence on model output content. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: if budget enforcement is entirely inside one pure function, Unit may be sufficient. TOP2-CONTRAST (runner-up Unit): behavior text includes "before dispatch," so test should verify over-budget inputs are blocked/adjusted at module-to-client boundary. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because no behavior text requires real network/subprocess.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a low budget, pass an over-budget document, and assert the module either truncates or rejects per policy and only dispatches payloads that satisfy budget constraints.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA and LLM-DEP: behavior requires a real provider call and real provider-reported token accounting; a stub can always fake compliance and miss real overages due to provider tokenizer/model behavior. This passes through a real AI/ML API call, so Agentic is the safe floor. MULTI-STEP: single call, so not Workflow. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: if the goal were only local estimator math, Integration would suffice. TOP2-CONTRAST (runner-up System): although real HTTP is involved, the critical failure source is provider model/tokenization behavior, not generic transport mechanics. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly depends on provider-reported usage.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real classification calls for representative documents and assert reported prompt/completion/total token usage remains <= configured budget per call.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is retry orchestration across modules (error classifier, backoff policy, retrier, caller contract). REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced with a fake provider returning rate-limit responses and controllable clock; real network not strictly required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no model-output-quality dependency and no multi-agent chain. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: if retry timing relies on real HTTP client/socket timeout semantics, System may be needed. TOP2-CONTRAST (runner-up System): behavior text is protocol-level retry on rate-limit signal, which is testable via deterministic in-process stubs.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake provider that returns rate-limit error then success; assert module waits according to retry policy, retries, and ultimately returns success instead of surfacing immediate error.
